_schema_note: Feed this file to POST /eval/run as the 'cases' array. All expected_doc_id values are null until you run scripts/apply_doc_ids.py. doc_id and doc_ids are intentionally omitted so retrieval searches across ALL uploaded documents — this tests cross-document retrieval quality.

cases.query: What specific problem does DPR identify with BM25 and TF-IDF for open-domain question answering?
How does DPR train its dual-encoder using in-batch negative sampling?
What is the Wikipedia passage corpus used by DPR and how are passages chunked and indexed?
What top-20 retrieval accuracy does DPR report on the Natural Questions test set?
How does DPR's dual-encoder architecture differ from a cross-encoder at inference time?
What pre-trained model serves as the backbone for DPR's question and passage encoders?
How does ColBERT's late interaction mechanism score query-passage relevance differently from DPR's single-vector dot product?
What is the MaxSim operator in ColBERT and how is it used to compute the final query-passage relevance score?
What MRR@10 score does ColBERT achieve on the MS MARCO passage ranking development set?
How does ColBERT compress its token-level representations for scalable offline indexing?
What query augmentation technique does ColBERT apply before encoding queries and why?
What are the two RAG variants—RAG-Sequence and RAG-Token—and how do they differ in marginalizing over retrieved passages during generation?
How does the RAG model jointly fine-tune the generator while keeping the retriever frozen?
What retriever backbone and passage index does RAG use for document retrieval?
On which knowledge-intensive NLP tasks does RAG outperform closed-book GPT-2 and fine-tuned T5 baselines?
What is the main contribution of 'Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks' to open-domain question answering?
What limitations of the RAG approach are acknowledged in the RAG paper regarding factual consistency?
How many datasets does BEIR include and what retrieval task types does it cover?
Why do the BEIR authors argue that strong in-domain retrieval performance does not predict zero-shot generalization?
What average nDCG@10 does BM25 achieve across all BEIR datasets compared to SBERT and DPR?
What conclusion does BEIR draw about dense retrieval model generalization to biomedical and legal retrieval domains?
What is the main contribution of the BEIR benchmark to the information retrieval research community?
What span extraction format does SQuAD use for answers and how are answer spans verified across annotators?
How were SQuAD questions sourced from Wikipedia articles and what crowdsourcing platform was used?
What exact match and F1 scores do the SQuAD paper's logistic regression and neural baseline models achieve?
How does SQuAD v1.1 handle questions whose answer is not present in the passage?
What evaluation metrics does SQuAD use and what human performance ceiling does the paper report?
How does Natural Questions collect questions from real Google Search queries rather than crowd-sourced question creation?
What are the short answer and long answer formats defined in Natural Questions and how are they annotated?
What inter-annotator agreement is reported in the Natural Questions annotation study?
What F1 scores do BERT-based models achieve on Natural Questions short-answer extraction?
How does Natural Questions distinguish itself from SQuAD in question distribution and answer format?
What two-stage pipeline does DrQA use—document retriever and document reader—and how are they connected?
How does DrQA's document reader use bilinear attention to predict answer span start and end positions?
What TF-IDF bigram hashing retrieval step does DrQA apply before the neural reading comprehension model?
What exact match accuracy does DrQA report on SQuAD, WebQuestions, and CuratedTREC?
How does DrQA handle the train-test distribution mismatch when a model trained on SQuAD is applied to open-domain questions?
What is gap sentence generation (GSG) pre-training in PEGASUS and how are the gap sentences selected?
What three sentence selection strategies—lead, random, and principal—does PEGASUS compare for gap sentence generation?
What ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, and ROUGE-L scores does PEGASUS achieve on XSum compared to prior state-of-the-art models?
What pre-training corpus and how many documents does PEGASUS train on before summarization fine-tuning?
What is the main contribution of PEGASUS to abstractive summarization pre-training?
What five text corruption schemes does BART evaluate in its pre-training ablation?
What encoder-decoder architecture does BART use and how does it differ from BERT and GPT pre-training?
What ROUGE scores does BART achieve on CNN/DailyMail and XSum abstractive summarization benchmarks?
How does BART handle machine translation by adding a randomly initialized encoder for the source language?
Which noise function ablation results show that text-infilling is BART's most effective corruption strategy for generation tasks?
What is an atomic fact as defined in FActScore, and how are atomic facts decomposed from long-form generation?
What knowledge source does FActScore use for fact verification—retrieval from Wikipedia or a prompted LM—and why?
What factual precision scores do GPT-3, GPT-4, and ChatGPT achieve on FActScore's biography generation benchmark?
How does FActScore handle atomic facts that require temporal or numerical reasoning to verify?
What inter-annotator agreement level does FActScore achieve for its binary atomic fact annotation scheme?
What are BERT's two pre-training tasks—Masked Language Modeling and Next Sentence Prediction—and how does each task work?
How does BERT fine-tune for extractive QA: what output heads are added for span start and span end prediction?
What are the parameter counts of BERT-Base and BERT-Large and how many Transformer layers does each have?
What GLUE score does BERT-Large achieve and by how many points does it outperform prior models like ELMo and GPT?
How does BERT's WordPiece tokenizer split rare or unknown words into subword tokens?
What does BERT's ablation study reveal about the contribution of Next Sentence Prediction to downstream task performance?
What are the three distinct uses of attention in the Transformer: encoder self-attention, decoder self-attention, and cross-attention?
How does the Transformer's positional encoding use sine and cosine functions of different frequencies to encode position?
What are the model dimensionality, number of attention heads, and feed-forward layer size for the Transformer Base model?
What BLEU score does the Transformer Base model achieve on WMT 2014 English-German translation?
How does the Transformer's self-attention computational complexity compare to RNNs as a function of sequence length?
What is a chain-of-thought exemplar and how many few-shot examples are included in the chain-of-thought prompts?
What accuracy does chain-of-thought prompting with PaLM 540B achieve on the GSM8K math word problem benchmark?
How does chain-of-thought prompting interact with model scale for arithmetic and symbolic reasoning benchmarks?
What ablation does the chain-of-thought paper run to show that intermediate reasoning steps are necessary for accuracy gains?
How does chain-of-thought prompting perform on commonsense reasoning benchmarks like StrategyQA and ARC-Challenge?
What three training phases comprise the InstructGPT RLHF pipeline: SFT, reward model training, and PPO fine-tuning?
How is the reward model trained in InstructGPT and what pairwise comparison dataset is used?
What human preference rate does a 1.3B InstructGPT model achieve over the 175B GPT-3 model in labeler evaluations?
How does InstructGPT measure the alignment tax on academic NLP benchmarks and what performance degradation is observed?
What is the approximate size of the human preference comparison dataset used to train InstructGPT's reward model?
How does InstructGPT's PPO training incorporate a KL divergence penalty to prevent reward hacking?
What are the eight task categories in MT-Bench used to evaluate multi-turn conversational ability?
How does the LLM-as-judge framework distinguish pairwise comparison from single-answer point-wise scoring?
What agreement rate between GPT-4 judgments and human expert judgments does the LLM-as-judge paper report?
What position bias is identified in LLM-as-judge evaluation and what swap strategy is used to detect and mitigate it?
What is the Chatbot Arena ELO rating system and how does it aggregate pairwise human preferences across models?
How does MT-Bench's second-turn question format test model ability to handle follow-up instructions in a multi-turn conversation?
